title: Ensure public network access is Disabled

input_row.title: Ensure public network access is Disabled
input_row.description: Disable public network access to prevent exposure to the internet and reduce the risk of unauthorized access. Use private endpoints and Azure Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) to securely manage access within trusted networks.
input_row.rationale: Disabling public network access improves security by ensuring that a service is not exposed on the public internet.
input_row.remediation: Additional Information: This Common Reference Recommendation is referenced in the following Service Recommendations: • Storage Services > Storage Accounts > Networking > "Ensure that 'Public Network Access' is 'Disabled' for storage accounts" • Storage Services > Azure Elastic SAN > "Ensure 'Public network access' is set to 'Disabled' on Azure Elastic SAN" • Storage Services > Azure Backup > Recovery Services Vaults > "Ensure public network access on Recovery Services vaults is Disabled"
input_row.impact: Disabling public network access restricts access to the service. This enhances security but may require the configuration of private endpoints for any services or users needing access within trusted networks.
input_row.additional_information: This Common Reference Recommendation is referenced in the following Service Recommendations: • Storage Services > Storage Accounts > Networking > "Ensure that 'Public Network Access' is 'Disabled' for storage accounts" • Storage Services > Azure Elastic SAN > "Ensure 'Public network access' is set to 'Disabled' on Azure Elastic SAN" • Storage Services > Azure Backup > Recovery Services Vaults > "Ensure public network access on Recovery Services vaults is Disabled"

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:

- **AWS API Calls and boto3 Methods:**
  - Use `boto3.client('s3')` and call `get_public_access_block(Bucket='your-bucket-name')` to verify if public access is blocked for S3 buckets.
- **AWS CLI Commands:**
  - Use the command `aws s3api get-public-access-block --bucket your-bucket-name` to retrieve the public access block configuration for S3 buckets.
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Enumerate all S3 buckets in the account using `boto3.client('s3').list_buckets()`.
- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Check for the following properties in the public access block configuration:
    - `BlockPublicAcls` should be set to `True`.
    - `IgnorePublicAcls` should be set to `True`.
    - `BlockPublicPolicy` should be set to `True`.
    - `RestrictPublicBuckets` should be set to `True`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - Pass: All four properties mentioned above are set to `True`.
  - Fail: Any one or more of the above properties is set to `False`.
  
This approach ensures comprehensive coverage for evaluating if public access is appropriately disabled across AWS S3 buckets, aligning with the security intent outlined in the compliance control.
